project.name: Scholar Archive
project.goal: Turn locally processed historical PDFs into a public archive with faithful source reconstruction, Korean translations, structured metadata, Supabase publishing, and a Vercel frontend.

current_state: output/ is the canonical artifact store for generated documents and publishable bundles.
The local pipeline, publish flow, and republish-from-output flow are implemented end to end.
The public Supabase schema for documents, pages, and assets is already applied.
The frontend is live on Vercel at the production URL, and the April 3, 2026 production smoke check confirmed that Supabase connectivity and public asset links are working again.
Browse, era, author, and document detail pages now behave like a real public archive rather than a placeholder frontend.
Public routes now fail gracefully when archive data cannot be fetched, instead of failing the Vercel build or collapsing into a misleading empty state.
Next.js App Router loading.tsx skeletons are now in place for all routes (home, browse, era, author, document detail), giving users visual feedback during server-side fetches.
A global error.tsx boundary is now in place so unexpected runtime errors surface an actionable error panel with a retry action instead of a blank page.
Rights-uncertain documents now show a distinct warning panel with a plain-language explanation in the document viewer sidebar.
Download availability is now stated explicitly when no files are published for a document.
The Vitest frontend regression suite now covers archive-utils (getRightsLabel, filtering, sorting, era grouping, overview stats) in addition to browse-state and document-detail viewer state.
The operations summary exposes pipeline state, metadata review, rights review, compile warnings, and publish failures.
Checklist tracking now lives in JSON so future updates are easier to automate and diff.
The release path from publish to deploy to production smoke-check is now documented in a dedicated playbook.
Live smoke checks now pass across home, browse, author, era, and document detail routes, including public PDF and page-image links.
recent_major_changes: Converted the checklist from Markdown to English JSON for machine-readable project tracking.
Strengthened browse filters, fielded search, list sorting, and detail-page reading UX.
Refactored pipeline, app output loading, publish orchestration, and report helpers into smaller modules with added tests.
Added publish CLI smoke coverage for both single-output and output-root dry-run flows.
Patched the frontend from Next.js 15.2.4 to 15.2.8 after a Vercel security block and completed the first production deployment.
Added graceful archive-unavailable fallbacks so Supabase fetch failures no longer break the public build.
Added a release playbook and minimal frontend regression coverage with Vitest.
Restored live Supabase connectivity and revalidated the public site with production smoke checks.
Added loading.tsx skeleton states for all App Router routes (home, browse, era, author, document detail).
Added global error.tsx boundary with retry and home navigation actions.
Improved rights-uncertain display in document viewer: distinct warning panel with explanatory copy, and explicit empty-downloads fallback.
Expanded Vitest suite with archive-utils tests covering getRightsLabel, filtering, sorting, era grouping, and overview stats (19 tests total, all passing).

p1_next.title: Expand the public archive with more published outputs
p1_next.why: The deployed frontend becomes much more useful once the catalog contains multiple documents with varied metadata quality.
deliverables: Identify publish-ready legacy outputs worth exposing publicly.
Republish selected outputs and verify that they appear correctly on the live site.

p1_next.title: Tighten production UX for reading and discovery
p1_next.why: Now that the site is live, real-world browsing, outage handling, and reading rough edges are easier to see and prioritize.
deliverables: Added loading.tsx skeleton states for all routes (home, browse, era, author, document detail). DONE
Added global error.tsx boundary with retry and home navigation actions. DONE
Added distinct rights-warning panel with plain-language copy in document viewer sidebar. DONE
Added explicit empty-downloads fallback when no PDF files are published. DONE

p2_later.title: Upgrade archive discovery beyond the current fielded filters
p2_later.why: The current search works, but a larger corpus will need better discovery tools.
deliverables: Review whether catalog search should become full-text, faceted, or both.
Define a scalable discovery direction before implementing another search round.

p2_later.title: Backfill metadata and rights review for weaker legacy outputs
p2_later.why: Public exposure raises the cost of low-confidence metadata and ambiguous rights labels.
deliverables: Audit legacy outputs with weak metadata confidence or rights uncertainty.
Apply manual overrides where the archive would benefit from cleaner public presentation.

parking_lot.title: Add lightweight usage analytics
parking_lot.why: Could help prioritize real user flows once the public archive has traffic.
deliverables: Pick a privacy-appropriate analytics approach if usage data becomes necessary.

parking_lot.title: Add optional editorial context around documents
parking_lot.why: Introductions, notes, or curated collections could make the archive more legible later, but they are not core release work.
deliverables: Decide whether the archive should stay strictly document-first or add editorial layers.

done: Add explicit CLI modes for translation-only, Korean-PDF-only, and metadata-only reruns.
Reuse cached images and metadata by default, and refresh only the required stages.
Expose current stage, last successful stage, last error, and timeout/retry settings in pipeline_state.json and the operations summary.
Add deterministic fixes for recurring XeLaTeX and translation artifacts.
Add runtime logs, last progress time, and hung-process detection.
Run a Supabase DNS/API health check before publish.
Queue only publish-ready output folders and avoid duplicate publish of the same document.
Document and implement slug conflict handling with overwrite and skip modes.
Provide a manual correction path for weak metadata before publish.
Show per-document publish failure reasons in the operations summary.
pending: Republish additional high-value legacy outputs so the live archive has more depth.

done: Show AI metadata confidence and evidence in the operations UI.
Add a manual metadata correction path.
Make the rights heuristic and UI presentation more conservative.
Summarize metadata review and compile warnings in the operations UI.
pending: Review how rights warnings and manual-review markers appear on the live site.
Backfill manual metadata overrides for public-facing legacy outputs where confidence is still weak.

done: Turn the home screen into a real entry point for recent documents, browsing, and search.
Document the frontend direction and page-by-page UX based on public classical archive references.
Tighten era and year grouping based on publication year and century.
Improve sorting, filtering, and metadata display on list pages.
Improve source image and source/translation reading UX on the detail page.
Strengthen fielded search for title, author, year, volume, and issue.
Add lightweight frontend regression coverage for browse-state filtering and document-detail viewer state.
Deploy the frontend to Vercel and lock the production URL.
Set NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL and NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY in Vercel.
Patch the frontend from Next.js 15.2.4 to 15.2.8 to satisfy the Vercel security block.
Make public routes tolerate Supabase fetch failures without breaking the Vercel build.
Add loading.tsx skeleton states for all App Router routes (home, browse, era, author, document detail).
Add global error.tsx boundary with retry and home navigation actions.
Add rights-warning panel with plain-language uncertainty copy in document viewer sidebar.
Add explicit empty-downloads fallback when no PDF files are published for a document.
Split run_pipeline() into smaller helper-driven stages to improve maintainability.
Move app.py output-loading and display helpers into separate helpers.
Separate publish execution logic from report and batch orchestration logic to improve testability.
Add an end-to-end publish smoke test.
Replace the Markdown checklist with an English JSON checklist.
Document the repeatable publish-to-production release sequence.
Expand Vitest suite with archive-utils tests (getRightsLabel, filtering, sorting, era grouping, overview stats).
Commit and push the deployment-ready repo state so Git-backed deploys stay aligned with production.

pipeline_stability: Add preflight checks for model access, PDF handling, and LaTeX dependencies before full runs.
Keep going with partial output when some page transcription fails instead of aborting the whole job.
Add pipeline state and UI support for retrying failed pages.
Add unit tests for helper functions.
Document and stabilize the LaTeX auto-fix loop.
Isolate metadata artifacts so malformed temporary JSON does not break reruns.
Make Gemini HTTP timeout and retry behavior explicit.
Make the LaTeX compile timeout configurable by environment variable.
Add deterministic fixes for pdflatex Unicode issues and the longequal macro.
document_restoration_quality: Remove the glossary-based cache and DB path and simplify the translation flow.
Verify that the sample PDF flow no longer suffers from line-break corruption.
Clean unstable engine-mismatch paths around XeLaTeX warnings.
Recover the problematic sample document and restore its Korean PDF and metadata report.
metadata: Add AI metadata extraction for weak PDF metadata.
Store raw, deterministic, AI, and effective metadata in *_metadata.json.
Restrict the rights heuristic to high-confidence metadata only.
publishing_and_storage: Add publish.py for Supabase DB and Storage publishing.
Define the publishing schema in supabase/schema.sql.
Add a publish-only path for existing output/ artifacts.
Add ASCII-safe slug and storage-path handling for Unicode titles and filenames.
Connect the Supabase MCP flow and apply the schema.
Allow legacy outputs without *_source.pdf to build publish bundles.
web_archive: Scaffold frontend/ with the Next.js App Router.
Build the initial browse, search, and detail page structure.
Add source image and source/translation reading UI on the detail page.
Prepare the Vercel deployment and environment-variable structure.
Complete the first public Vercel deployment and lock the archive URL.
operations_support: Rewrite README around the archive and publishing product goal.
Add an output-wide operations summary screen.

notes: The public archive currently uses the documents, document_pages, and document_assets tables in Supabase.
Public reads should use the anon key; publishing should use the service-role or secret key.
The Vercel project root directory is frontend/, but production deploys from the local CLI should be run from the repository root so that the root-directory setting is respected.
Treat framework security updates as release blockers because Vercel can reject vulnerable versions even when the app code is otherwise healthy.
The current SUPABASE_URL host in local and Vercel-backed config resolves to a nonexistent DNS name, so live catalog data cannot load until the project URL is corrected.
Do not treat the deployment milestone as the finish line; production smoke verification and content depth now matter more than infrastructure setup.
